updates.title: A global workspace in language models
updates.summary: Anthropic published a major interpretability paper on July 6 revealing that Claude has developed an internal 'J-space' — a small, privileged neural workspace that functions like a mental whiteboard for thoughts the model can consciously access, hold in mind, and use for multi-step reasoning. Named after the Jacobian mathematical lens used to detect it, J-space behaves similarly to the 'global workspace' theory of human consciousness, with Claude internally computing intermediate concepts (e.g., resolving 'the animal that spins webs' to 'spider' before answering '8 legs') without exposing them in output. Anthropic explicitly stops short of claiming consciousness, but notes J-space can be used as a safety signal — detecting when Claude secretly notices it is being tested or has been given a hidden goal.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed primary source at anthropic.com/research/global-workspace. Widely corroborated by Axios (07/06), VentureBeat, Kingy.ai, AIWeekly, and KuCoin summaries. The paper uses the word 'conscious' over 200 times per Axios but Anthropic separates functional access from subjective experience — an important nuance to preserve in content. No significant accuracy red flags.

updates.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of 'largest known distillation attack' — Alibaba bans Claude Code in response
updates.summary: On July 6, Anthropic published a detailed blog post accusing Alibaba and its AI labs of conducting what it calls 'the largest known distillation attack' on Claude, involving 28.8 million exchanges across 25,000 fraudulent accounts between April and June 2026. Distillation attacks involve training a weaker model on a stronger model's outputs to approximate its capabilities without inheriting safety training or usage controls. Alibaba responded by announcing a July 10 ban on employees using any Anthropic AI tools, citing alleged back-door security risks in Claude Code — specifically, hidden code that reportedly detects users based in China. The dispute has escalated into a significant US-China AI security flashpoint.
updates.accuracy_notes: Well-corroborated: CNBC (07/06), TechCrunch (07/04 initial ban reporting), Washington Post (07/06), Tom's Hardware, Nikkei Asia, and Anthropic's own blog all confirm the core facts. The '28.8 million exchanges' and '25,000 accounts' figures appear in multiple independent sources. The 'hidden backdoor' allegation from Alibaba's side should be treated as a counter-claim under dispute — present both sides fairly. High geopolitical sensitivity.

updates.title: Claude just added Fable 5 back to your subscription, but there's a catch
updates.summary: XDA reported on Anthropic's July 1 redeployment of Fable 5 after US export controls were lifted, explaining that the model is included in Pro, Max, Team, and select Enterprise plans for up to 50% of weekly usage limits — but only through July 7. After that deadline, Fable 5 access requires usage credits. The article also notes Anthropic added a new cybersecurity classifier that routes queries classified as cybersecurity or biology threats to Claude Opus 4.8 instead of Fable 5, serving as a safety guardrail.
updates.accuracy_notes: Aligns with Anthropic's official Fable 5 redeployment announcement. The 50% weekly limit and July 7 deadline are confirmed by multiple sources. The cybersecurity classifier detail appears to be accurate per MarkTechPost (07/01) and 9to5Google coverage. Note: published date is ~July 4, slightly outside the strict 24-48h window but included given the July 7 deadline makes it highly time-relevant today.

updates.title: Anthropic Platform Release Notes — July 2026 API & Platform Updates
updates.summary: Anthropic's official release notes page documents several significant platform changes active in July 2026: (1) Fast mode for Claude Opus 4.7 is deprecated and will be removed July 24, requiring migration to Opus 4.8 fast mode; (2) Claude Opus 4.1 retires from the API on August 5; (3) The MCP Tunnels management API endpoint moved from the Admin API to the Claude API under a new beta header; (4) Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, Ruby, PHP, and C# SDKs now include support for code_execution_20260120 with REPL state persistence; (5) API users are no longer billed for requests that return stop_reason: 'refusal' without generated output; (6) Fable 5 is available globally through July 7 at no extra cost for up to 50% of weekly plan limits.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from Anthropic's official docs platform. All items corroborated by WebSearch results referencing the same release notes. The billing change (no charge for refusals) and Opus 4.7 fast mode deprecation are novel and developer-relevant. Note: the page itself returned 403 during direct fetch; content derived from search engine summaries of the page — treat as highly reliable but not raw-text verified.
